description_long: This is a stone obelisk with a rectangular shaft, supported at each corner by slender cylindrical columns. The shaft is inscribed on all four sides with lines of ancient script, likely Greek, etched into a lighter stone panel set within the main structure. The obelisk stands on a broad, square base with beveled edges, giving it a sturdy and monumental appearance. The top is capped with a slightly projecting, flat stone slab, adding to its classical architectural style. The surface is weathered, with a mottled pattern of beige, gray, and brown tones, suggesting age and exposure to the elements.
description: A weathered stone obelisk with inscribed panels and four supporting columns, standing on a broad square base and capped with a flat stone slab.
description_view_0: Front view showing the inscribed panel framed by two columns and the prominent square base.
description_view_1: Side view with a similar inscribed panel and columns, but the stone texture and weathering are more pronounced.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying another inscribed panel, with the columns and base visible; inscriptions appear slightly more faded.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again showing the inscribed panel and columns, with consistent design elements across all sides.
attribute_inscription: All four sides feature vertical panels with engraved text in an ancient script, likely Greek, with moderate legibility due to weathering.
attribute_ornamentation: None visible beyond the structural columns and inscriptions.
attribute_base_design: The base is a broad, square platform with beveled edges, constructed from the same stone as the shaft, providing stability and a monumental appearance.
attribute_tip_design: The tip is capped with a flat, slightly projecting stone slab, rectangular in shape, with no additional decoration.
